plang.note: Builder requires --llmservice=openai, not available in this environment

coverage.note: dotnet-coverage profiler failed to initialize. Manual code-path analysis performed.

findings.issue: Resolve<T> does (T)value — a hard unboxing cast. C# unboxing requires exact type match: (long)(object)42 throws InvalidCastException when 42 is boxed as int. All tests store values as long literals, so the cast always succeeds.
findings.impact: First production use with a JSON-deserialized int parameter (e.g., 20971520 fits in int32) resolved as long will crash at runtime.
findings.suggestion: Add test: store int, resolve as long. Then fix cast to use Convert.ChangeType or safe pattern.

findings.issue: RunAsync saves/restores context.SettingsScope in try/finally (goal scoping). Tests only use CreateChild() which creates a new context with Parent chain — a completely different isolation mechanism. If lines 29-32 and 89 were deleted, no test fails.
findings.impact: Settings could leak between goals when same context is passed to sequential goal executions. Core goal-scoping behavior is unverified.
findings.suggestion: Write test: run Goal A (sets setting), run Goal B (same context) — B should NOT see A's setting.

findings.issue: Scope chain walk has 'if (current.SettingsScope != null)' skip logic, but only 2-level parent chains are tested. No test has a gap (middle parent with null SettingsScope).
findings.impact: A bug where null SettingsScope terminates the walk instead of skipping would go undetected.
findings.suggestion: Add 3-level test: grandparent(scope) → parent(no scope) → child. Child should resolve grandparent's value.

findings.issue: No test verifies that setting the same key twice updates the value. The overwrite contract is undocumented in tests.
findings.impact: Low — ConcurrentDictionary handles this correctly. But test should document the expected behavior.

findings.issue: Scope.Set(key, null) will throw ArgumentNullException (ConcurrentDictionary constraint). No way to unset a value. Hidden constraint, not tested or documented.
findings.impact: Low — settings are typically write-once per scope. But unexpected ArgumentNullException if someone tries to clear a setting.
findings.suggestion: Document in test or add null guard in Set() that either no-ops or removes the key.

findings.issue: Only one PLang test exists (set max gzip size). Missing tests for: engine defaults, compression level (enum), settings inheritance across goal calls.
findings.impact: Builder's ability to parse diverse settings steps is unverified. Only the happy path for one numeric setting is covered.
findings.suggestion: Add PLang tests: set default max gzip size, set compression level to fastest, test inheritance.

summary: 1254 C# tests pass. 15 settings-specific tests cover all happy paths through the scope chain (class default → engine default → goal scope → parent inheritance → child override). However, two critical gaps exist: (1) Resolve<T>'s hard unboxing cast will throw InvalidCastException on numeric type widening — a real production scenario with JSON-deserialized parameters — and no test guards against it; (2) the goal-scoped save/restore mechanism in Goal.RunAsync is completely untested — tests only exercise the CreateChild parent-chain mechanism, which is a different isolation strategy. Three minor findings round out the report.
